Notes:



Counts are unweighted MEPS 2022 person records at each derivation stage.


Medicare status is identified from the 2022 Full-Year Consolidated File before applying analytic exclusions.


Nonpositive full-year person weight and missing or invalid adult BMI are removed before the age restriction in this displayed flow so that the subsequent age exclusion shows the loss among Medicare records otherwise eligible for weighted BMI-based analyses.


Beneficiaries younger than 65 years, including disabled or ESRD Medicare beneficiaries aged 18-64 years, are removed after the weight and BMI restriction.


This flow does not exclude beneficiaries because of observed Medicare Part D status. Part D status is retained for the descriptive Part D versus non-Part D comparison in Table 1 and for Bridge/IRA policy eligibility in Tables 3a-3c.


The final cohort requires valid observed adult BMI because FDA GLP-1 weight-management eligibility depends on BMI thresholds.


The FDA GLP-1 weight-management proxy is BMI >=30, or BMI 27-29.9 plus at least one observable weight-related comorbidity proxy: hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, or cardiovascular disease.


CMS Medicare Bridge eligibility is not the analytic denominator. Bridge eligibility is applied later as a policy overlay among Part D beneficiaries using Bridge tier proxies and Bridge exclusion proxies.

Notes:



Abbreviations: FPL, Federal Poverty Level; GLP-1RA, glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist; MEPS, Medical Expenditure Panel Survey.


Population: Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in MEPS 2022 with valid observed adult BMI who met the FDA obesity/chronic weight-management GLP-1 eligibility proxy.


Columns compare beneficiaries with any observed Medicare Part D prescription drug coverage during the 2022 MEPS reference periods with Medicare beneficiaries who had no observed Part D coverage during those periods.


Estimates use the MEPS full-year person weight. Table 1 describes the Medicare aged >=65 FDA-eligible analytic population and is intentionally split by observed Part D status. Tables 3a-3c use the same FDA-eligible Medicare denominator regardless of Part D status.


The FDA eligibility proxy is BMI >=30, or BMI 27-29.9 plus at least one observable weight-related comorbidity proxy: hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, or cardiovascular disease.


Bridge tier proxies are retained as clinical context but do not define the overall analytic denominator. Bridge Tier 1 is BMI >=35. Bridge Tier 2 is BMI 30-34.9 plus heart failure, hypertension, or chronic kidney disease. Bridge Tier 3 is BMI 27-29.9 plus prediabetes, prior myocardial infarction, prior stroke, or peripheral artery disease.


Hypertension is defined by a MEPS survey hypertension diagnosis or hypertension CCSR condition coding. Heart failure is defined by a MEPS survey heart failure response or heart failure CCSR condition coding and represents any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction. Chronic kidney disease is based on chronic kidney disease CCSR condition coding and is not confirmed stage 3a or higher.


Prediabetes is based on ICD-10 R73. Prior myocardial infarction and prior stroke are based on MEPS survey diagnosis responses. Peripheral artery disease is based on ICD-10 I70, I73, or I74, or relevant circulatory CCSR condition coding, and is not confirmed symptomatic peripheral artery disease.


Bridge exclusion proxies are type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease. In the Bridge simulation, these variables route a beneficiary away from Bridge pricing and toward regular Part D coverage when Part D is observed. Type 2 diabetes is defined by a MEPS diabetes survey response, ICD-10 E11, or type 2 diabetes CCSR condition coding. The obstructive sleep apnea proxy uses ICD-10 G47 sleep-disorder coding, and the MASH/liver proxy uses ICD-10 K76 liver disease coding; neither is a clinical adjudication of moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea or noncirrhotic MASH.


Income categories are based on the Federal Poverty Level.

Abbreviations: CI, confidence interval; GLP-1RA, glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist; IRA, Inflation Reduction Act; MEPS, Medical Expenditure Panel Survey; OOP, out-of-pocket.


Population: Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in MEPS 2022 with valid observed adult BMI who met the FDA obesity/chronic weight-management GLP-1 eligibility proxy, regardless of observed Part D status.


Medicare Part D coverage means prescription drug benefit coverage observed at any point during the 2022 MEPS reference periods.


The Overall row includes the full FDA-eligible Medicare analytic cohort. The three BMI rows are mutually exclusive and are defined by BMI alone: BMI >=35, BMI 30-34.9, and BMI 27-29.9. Because the analytic cohort is FDA eligible, beneficiaries in the BMI 27-29.9 row also have at least one FDA weight-related comorbidity proxy.


The FDA eligibility proxy is BMI >=30, or BMI 27-29.9 plus at least one observable weight-related comorbidity proxy: hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, or cardiovascular disease.


Estimates use the MEPS full-year person weight for consistency across Table 1, Tables 3a-3c, the PMM model, and the supplementary outputs.


Baseline reflects observed annual OOP health care spending. Universal reflects simulated full-year GLP-1RA therapy for the FDA-eligible Medicare analytic cohort.


The Excl. Drug Cost, Baseline, and Universal columns are reference estimates calculated for the full population in the row shown.


† The dagger applies to the four columns under each policy scenario. Revised OOP, benefiting weighted n/N, and benefiting percentage use the full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown, regardless of observed Part D status. Beneficiary Savings uses the benefiting subset, as described below.


Medicare Bridge pricing is applied only to Bridge-eligible proxy beneficiaries within that denominator. Beneficiaries who are not Bridge eligible remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost in the Bridge scenario.


The Bridge proxy requires Part D enrollment, age >=65 years in this analysis, a Bridge tier pathway, and no observable Part D-coverable GLP-1 indication that would route the person to regular Part D coverage instead of Bridge pricing.


The Bridge proxy uses observable MEPS condition information rather than adjudicated clinical criteria. Heart failure is any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction; hypertension is observable hypertension, not confirmed uncontrolled hypertension; chronic kidney disease is observable CKD, not confirmed stage 3a or higher; and peripheral artery disease is observable PAD, not confirmed symptomatic PAD.


Inflation Reduction Act mechanics are applied only to beneficiaries with observed Part D coverage. Non-Part-D beneficiaries remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost in the IRA scenario. Exceeding the $2,100 Part D OOP cap determines who benefits financially.


The IRA and Bridge scenario assigns each beneficiary the lower-cost GLP-1RA route available within reason: regular Part D coverage subject to the IRA cap versus Bridge pricing when Bridge eligible. A beneficiary cannot receive GLP-1RA through both IRA and Bridge pathways simultaneously. Non-Part-D beneficiaries remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost.


In Table 3b, revised aggregate OOP is a national aggregate estimate over the full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown. Aggregate beneficiary savings are national aggregate savings among beneficiaries who benefit under the policy scenario.


Benefiting, weighted n/N reports the survey-weighted number of beneficiaries with lower OOP spending under the policy scenario divided by the survey-weighted full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown. Benefiting, % is the same weighted numerator divided by the same weighted denominator, with a 95% CI.


Beneficiary Savings is estimated only among beneficiaries who benefit under the policy scenario, not among the full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator. In Table 3a this is mean savings among beneficiaries who benefit; in Table 3b this is national aggregate savings among beneficiaries who benefit.


Standalone policy scenarios are mutually exclusive. Medicare Bridge caps GLP-1RA OOP at $600/year only among Bridge-eligible beneficiaries. IRA caps regular Part D prescription-drug OOP at $2,100/year without Bridge pricing.


Bridge exclusion proxies are type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease. Type 2 diabetes uses a MEPS diabetes survey response, ICD-10 E11, or type 2 diabetes CCSR condition coding. Sleep apnea uses ICD-10 G47 sleep-disorder coding as a specific observable proxy rather than adjudicated moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea. MASH/liver disease uses ICD-10 K76 liver disease coding as a specific observable proxy rather than confirmed noncirrhotic MASH.


Costs are reported in January 2026 US dollars. Confidence intervals for costs use bootstrap-estimated standard errors with 2,000 replicates and log transformation.


Estimates marked with an asterisk are statistically unreliable by 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards.

Population: Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in MEPS 2022 with valid observed adult BMI who met the FDA obesity/chronic weight-management GLP-1 eligibility proxy, regardless of observed Part D status.


Medicare Part D coverage means prescription drug benefit coverage observed at any point during the 2022 MEPS reference periods.


The Overall row includes the full FDA-eligible Medicare analytic cohort. The three BMI rows are mutually exclusive and are defined by BMI alone: BMI >=35, BMI 30-34.9, and BMI 27-29.9. Because the analytic cohort is FDA eligible, beneficiaries in the BMI 27-29.9 row also have at least one FDA weight-related comorbidity proxy.


The FDA eligibility proxy is BMI >=30, or BMI 27-29.9 plus at least one observable weight-related comorbidity proxy: hypertension, dyslipidemia, type 2 diabetes, obstructive sleep apnea, or cardiovascular disease.


Financial toxicity is defined as annual OOP health care expenditures, including premiums, exceeding 20% of post-subsistence income.


Estimates use the MEPS full-year person weight for consistency across Table 1, Tables 3a-3c, the PMM model, and the supplementary outputs.


Baseline reflects observed annual OOP health care spending. Universal reflects simulated full-year GLP-1RA therapy for the FDA-eligible Medicare analytic cohort.


The Excl. Drug Cost, Baseline, and Universal columns are reference FT estimates calculated for the full population in the row shown.


† The dagger applies to the revised FT, benefiting weighted n/N, and benefiting percentage under each policy scenario. These scenario columns use the full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown, regardless of observed Part D status.


Medicare Bridge pricing is applied only to Bridge-eligible proxy beneficiaries within that denominator. Beneficiaries who are not Bridge eligible remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost in the Bridge scenario.


The Bridge proxy requires Part D enrollment, age >=65 years in this analysis, a Bridge tier pathway, and no observable Part D-coverable GLP-1 indication that would route the person to regular Part D coverage instead of Bridge pricing.


The Bridge proxy uses observable MEPS condition information rather than adjudicated clinical criteria. Heart failure is any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction; hypertension is observable hypertension, not confirmed uncontrolled hypertension; chronic kidney disease is observable CKD, not confirmed stage 3a or higher; and peripheral artery disease is observable PAD, not confirmed symptomatic PAD.


Inflation Reduction Act mechanics are applied only to beneficiaries with observed Part D coverage. Non-Part-D beneficiaries remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost in the IRA scenario. Exceeding the $2,100 Part D OOP cap determines who benefits financially.


The IRA and Bridge scenario assigns each beneficiary the lower-cost GLP-1RA route available within reason: regular Part D coverage subject to the IRA cap versus Bridge pricing when Bridge eligible. A beneficiary cannot receive GLP-1RA through both IRA and Bridge pathways simultaneously. Non-Part-D beneficiaries remain at their Universal GLP-1RA OOP cost.


Revised FT is estimated over the full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown. Benefiting, weighted n/N reports the survey-weighted number of beneficiaries with lower OOP spending under the policy scenario divided by the survey-weighted full FDA-eligible Medicare denominator in the row shown. Benefiting, % is the same weighted numerator divided by the same weighted denominator, with a 95% CI.


Standalone policy scenarios are mutually exclusive. Medicare Bridge caps GLP-1RA OOP at $600/year only among Bridge-eligible beneficiaries. IRA caps regular Part D prescription-drug OOP at $2,100/year without Bridge pricing.


Bridge exclusion proxies are type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease. Type 2 diabetes uses a MEPS diabetes survey response, ICD-10 E11, or type 2 diabetes CCSR condition coding. Sleep apnea uses ICD-10 G47 sleep-disorder coding as a specific observable proxy rather than adjudicated moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea. MASH/liver disease uses ICD-10 K76 liver disease coding as a specific observable proxy rather than confirmed noncirrhotic MASH.


Confidence intervals for proportions use Korn-Graubard beta intervals based on bootstrap-estimated standard errors with 2,000 replicates.


Estimates marked with an asterisk are statistically unreliable by 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards.


The Excl. Drug Cost column sets observed OOP costs for GLP-1RA therapy to zero.

Abbreviations: CI, confidence interval; GLP-1RA, glucagon-like peptide-1 receptor agonist; IRA, Inflation Reduction Act; MEPS, Medical Expenditure Panel Survey; OOP, out-of-pocket; SGLT2i, sodium-glucose cotransporter 2 inhibitor.


Population: Medicare Part D beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in MEPS 2022 with valid observed adult BMI who met the observable BMI/comorbidity pathway used to approximate CMS Medicare GLP-1 Bridge clinical criteria.


Medicare Part D coverage means prescription drug benefit coverage observed at any point during the 2022 MEPS reference periods.


The Overall row includes the full Medicare Part D analytic cohort, which is already restricted to beneficiaries meeting the observable Bridge BMI/comorbidity pathway. The three BMI rows are mutually exclusive and are defined by BMI only: BMI >=35, BMI 30-34.9, and BMI 27-29.9. The BMI rows are not further restricted by the additional comorbidity requirements used to determine whether an individual is Bridge eligible.


Estimates use the MEPS full-year person weight for consistency across Table 1, Tables 3a-3c, the PMM model, and the supplementary outputs.


Baseline reflects observed annual OOP health care spending. Universal reflects simulated full-year GLP-1RA therapy for the Medicare Part D analytic cohort plus targeted full-year SGLT2i therapy only among beneficiaries with type 2 diabetes, heart failure, or chronic kidney disease.


The Excl. Drug Cost, Baseline, and Universal columns are reference estimates calculated for the full population in the row shown.


† The dagger applies to the four columns under each policy scenario. These scenario columns use the population that could realistically be considered for that scenario, not necessarily the full row population.


Cost Plus SGLT2i columns are estimated among beneficiaries eligible for targeted SGLT2i therapy, defined as observable type 2 diabetes, heart failure, or chronic kidney disease.


Medicare Bridge columns are estimated among Bridge-eligible proxy beneficiaries. The Bridge proxy requires Part D enrollment, age >=65 years in this analysis, the observable BMI/comorbidity pathway, and no observable Part D-coverable GLP-1 indication that would route the person to regular Part D coverage instead of Bridge pricing.


The Bridge proxy uses observable MEPS condition information rather than adjudicated clinical criteria. Heart failure is any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction; hypertension is observable hypertension, not confirmed uncontrolled hypertension; chronic kidney disease is observable CKD, not confirmed stage 3a or higher; and peripheral artery disease is observable PAD, not confirmed symptomatic PAD.


Inflation Reduction Act columns are estimated among all Medicare Part D beneficiaries in the row. Exceeding the $2,100 Part D OOP cap determines who benefits financially; it is not an eligibility restriction for the IRA denominator.


Combined optimized columns are estimated among all Medicare Part D beneficiaries in the row because every beneficiary can be considered under the IRA Part D cap framework, and Cost Plus SGLT2i may apply when clinically eligible.


Revised OOP is estimated among the scenario-applicable denominator for that policy scenario. In Table 3b, revised aggregate OOP is the national aggregate within that same scenario-applicable denominator.


Benefiting, weighted n/N reports the survey-weighted number of beneficiaries with lower OOP spending under the policy scenario divided by the survey-weighted scenario-applicable denominator. Benefiting, % is the same weighted numerator divided by the same weighted denominator, with a 95% CI.


Beneficiary Savings is estimated only among beneficiaries who benefit under that scenario, not among the full scenario-applicable denominator. For Table 3b, savings are national aggregate savings among beneficiaries who benefit.


Standalone policy scenarios are mutually exclusive. Cost Plus SGLT2i caps targeted SGLT2i OOP at $120/year where applicable. Medicare Bridge caps GLP-1RA OOP at $600/year only among Bridge-eligible beneficiaries. IRA caps regular Part D prescription-drug OOP at $2,100/year without Bridge pricing.


The combined optimized scenario applies Cost Plus SGLT2i where eligible and then assigns each person the lower-cost GLP-1RA route within reason: Bridge pricing when Bridge eligible versus regular Part D coverage subject to the IRA cap. A person cannot receive GLP-1RA through both Bridge and IRA pathways simultaneously.


Bridge exclusion proxies are type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease. Type 2 diabetes uses a MEPS diabetes survey response, ICD-10 E11, or type 2 diabetes CCSR condition coding. Sleep apnea uses ICD-10 G47 sleep-disorder coding as a specific observable proxy rather than adjudicated moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea. MASH/liver disease uses ICD-10 K76 liver disease coding as a specific observable proxy rather than confirmed noncirrhotic MASH.


Costs are reported in January 2026 US dollars. Confidence intervals for costs use bootstrap-estimated standard errors with 2,000 replicates and log transformation.


Estimates marked with an asterisk are statistically unreliable by 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards.

Inflation Reduction Act columns are estimated among all Medicare Part D beneficiaries in the row. Exceeding the $2,100 Part D OOP cap determines who benefits financially; it is not an eligibility restriction for the IRA denominator.


Combined optimized columns are estimated among all Medicare Part D beneficiaries in the row because every beneficiary can be considered under the IRA Part D cap framework, and Cost Plus SGLT2i may apply when clinically eligible.


Revised FT is estimated among the scenario-applicable denominator for that policy scenario. Benefiting, weighted n/N reports the survey-weighted number of beneficiaries with lower OOP spending under the policy scenario divided by the survey-weighted scenario-applicable denominator. Benefiting, % is the same weighted numerator divided by the same weighted denominator, with a 95% CI.


Standalone policy scenarios are mutually exclusive. Cost Plus SGLT2i caps targeted SGLT2i OOP at $120/year where applicable. Medicare Bridge caps GLP-1RA OOP at $600/year only among Bridge-eligible beneficiaries. IRA caps regular Part D prescription-drug OOP at $2,100/year without Bridge pricing.


The combined optimized scenario applies Cost Plus SGLT2i where eligible and then assigns each person the lower-cost GLP-1RA route within reason: Bridge pricing when Bridge eligible versus regular Part D coverage subject to the IRA cap. A person cannot receive GLP-1RA through both Bridge and IRA pathways simultaneously.


Bridge exclusion proxies are type 2 diabetes, sleep apnea, and MASH/liver disease. Type 2 diabetes uses a MEPS diabetes survey response, ICD-10 E11, or type 2 diabetes CCSR condition coding. Sleep apnea uses ICD-10 G47 sleep-disorder coding as a specific observable proxy rather than adjudicated moderate-to-severe obstructive sleep apnea. MASH/liver disease uses ICD-10 K76 liver disease coding as a specific observable proxy rather than confirmed noncirrhotic MASH.


Confidence intervals for proportions use Korn-Graubard beta intervals based on bootstrap-estimated standard errors with 2,000 replicates.


Estimates marked with an asterisk are statistically unreliable by 2017 National Center for Health Statistics standards.


The Excl. Drug Cost column sets observed OOP costs for the targeted therapies to zero.

The Bridge proxy requires Part D enrollment, age >=65 years in this analysis, a Bridge tier pathway, and no observable Part D-coverable GLP-1 indication that would route the person to regular Part D coverage instead of Bridge pricing.


The Bridge proxy uses observable MEPS condition information rather than adjudicated clinical criteria. Heart failure is any observable heart failure, not confirmed heart failure with preserved ejection fraction; hypertension is observable hypertension, not confirmed uncontrolled hypertension; chronic kidney disease is observable CKD, not confirmed stage 3a or higher; and peripheral artery disease is observable PAD, not confirmed symptomatic PAD.

Table: annualized GLP-1RA OOP cost distributions, shown as weighted population size, median, IQR, and 10th-90th percentile range.


Donor pool: observed Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older with valid observed adult BMI who used GLP-1RA therapy and had nonmissing daily OOP costs. Donors may or may not have observed Part D coverage, because imputation is stratified to the Medicare insurance stratum rather than to Part D enrollment alone.


Predictive mean matching used random forests. Predictors included age, continuous adult BMI, sex, race and ethnicity, census region, family income, federal poverty level percentage, background prescription-drug OOP spending, premium OOP spending, inpatient OOP spending, outpatient/office-based OOP spending, observed Part D status, and the clinical proxies listed in Table 1a.


Observed recipient users: FDA-eligible Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in the policy recipient cohort who were already using GLP-1RA therapy.


Simulated recipients: FDA-eligible Medicare beneficiaries aged 65 years or older in the analytic cohort after assigning observed or imputed GLP-1RA daily costs.


Estimates use the MEPS full-year person weight.


Costs are annualized by multiplying daily OOP cost by 365 and are reported in January 2026 US dollars.
